title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Evaluation Brief: Relevant Context
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt relevant context increment in a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Relevant Context
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Relevant Context contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.

content.text: Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Relevant Context, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template.

steps.title: Build Relevant Context evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-relevant-context-gaow1u" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Relevant Context into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-relevant-context-gaow1u
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Relevant Context model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Relevant Context applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
